title: Thelypteris costularisora
intro: Thelypteris costularis, also known as the Mexican fan fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and gastrointestinal relief. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris costularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Thelypteris costularis is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
